note: Repaired successor to pass-1 candidate-1. No rule here is creator-owned; nothing in this contract certifies literary quality. failureShape/passShape are structural descriptions by design — this pass contains no example sentences. candidate-2.1 (gate repair 2026-07-17): (1) ModelNarrationOutput and NarrationPipelineEnvelope split into separate root schemas; (2) AC-DLG-01 added — licensed_dialogue binds only to typed speech events or pre-issued speech_act licenses, never to general resolved events. candidate-2.2 (2026-07-18, creator-approved minimal mapping): five rule-local severity/owner fields aligned to severityMatrix — no other rule statement, schema, prose policy, or scene behavior changed. Executable split preserved: preflight 18 / post-validator 12 / heuristic 1 / human-owned 5.

transferableTechniques.technique: Emotion carried by an authorized physical response instead of an explanatory emotion label
transferableTechniques.applicabilityRule: Usable only when a resolved event or license authorizes the physical response itself
transferableTechniques.imitationBoundary: Technique only; no wording or gesture vocabulary from the reference may be reproduced

transferableTechniques.technique: An authorized object or spatial feature reveals character or alters the action
transferableTechniques.applicabilityRule: Object must be a registered anchor doing causal work; decorative objects are not added

transferableTechniques.technique: Dialogue alters information, action, relationship, or commitment in the present beat
transferableTechniques.applicabilityRule: Requires dialogueAuthority.mode = licensed with an enumerated speech act

transferableTechniques.technique: Theme produced by accumulated action and consequence, never taught by a character
transferableTechniques.applicabilityRule: Cross-scene property; no single sentence may state the theme

transferableTechniques.technique: Sensory transition used only when the source authorizes the sensation and the transition has a scene function
transferableTechniques.applicabilityRule: Requires a sensory_detail license or an event-sourced sensation

forbiddenConstructions.name: theme_teaching_dialogue
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: A licensed_dialogue sentence whose plain intent explains the theme, the rules of the world, or the speaker's own inner state as commentary rather than performing an enumerated speech act

forbiddenConstructions.name: quotable_line_seeking
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: A sentence with no bound plan role whose form is a general-truth assertion detached from present actors, objects, or events

forbiddenConstructions.name: cryptic_aphorism
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: Dialogue shaped as riddle, maxim, or symbolic slogan; plain intent cannot be stated as one of the enumerated speech acts without residue

forbiddenConstructions.name: personified_abstraction
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: An abstract noun or place as grammatical agent of a volitional verb in narrator voice, especially as a scene opener

forbiddenConstructions.name: surrogate_speaker_metaphor
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: A construction in which a body part, object, or abstraction is said to speak, answer, or decide on behalf of a character

forbiddenConstructions.name: ornamental_inversion
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: Marked departure from canonical English constituent order used for elevation rather than information structure

forbiddenConstructions.name: fragment_afterthought
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: A verbless or subjectless fragment appended after a complete sentence for lingering effect in narrator voice
forbiddenConstructions.origin: craft-reference failure mode; parallel class exists in the creator's Korean harness as a dated creator correction — the English entry is a parallel construction, not a translation

forbiddenConstructions.name: fake_archaism
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: Archaic diction, epic epithets, or period syntax absent from the style profile's allowed register
forbiddenConstructions.origin: pass-1 finding retained; craft-reference failure mode

forbiddenConstructions.name: nominal_abstraction_translationese
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: Chains of abstract nominalizations with eventive passives erasing available agents, producing report register in narrator voice
forbiddenConstructions.origin: pass-1 finding retained; craft-reference failure mode; parallel class in the creator's Korean harness

forbiddenConstructions.name: mirrored_wrapup
forbiddenConstructions.detectionCriterion: Perfectly symmetric scene assembly or an explicit internal conclusion that resolves the scene's meaning for the reader

rules.statement: renderDescriptor, renderText, and observableText are authored before the model call by the creator or a registered deterministic transform only; copying raw engine summary, analytic explanation, or publicDescription wording into them is a hard fail
rules.rationale: Pass-1 root cause: engine-register input became prose diction
rules.failureShape: A camera-safe field containing engine-inspection phrasing or a verbatim run from a raw engine record
rules.passShape: A camera-safe field stating only what a camera or the focal character could register, bound to source IDs

rules.statement: The renderer model may not create, edit, extend, or reinterpret any camera-safe field; it consumes them as constraints
rules.rationale: Separates representation authorship from rendering
rules.failureShape: Model output introducing content absent from every camera-safe field, license, and event
rules.passShape: All prose content traceable to supplied fields via the plan receipt

rules.statement: Analytic vocabulary and engine field names are forbidden inside camera-safe fields at authoring time
rules.rationale: Leak prevention must happen at the seam, not after generation
rules.failureShape: A renderText carrying epistemic-report nouns or schema field names
rules.passShape: Concrete perceptual wording only

rules.statement: authorizedActionEventIds, authorizedReactionEventIds, and authorizedChangeEventIds must each be a subset of resolvedEvents[].eventId
rules.rationale: Authority cannot exceed what the engine resolved
rules.failureShape: An authorized list citing an eventId absent from resolvedEvents
rules.passShape: Every authorized ID resolvable to a supplied event

rules.statement: Every sentence plan must bind to at least one existing source fact, source event, or pre-issued license; plans citing unknown IDs are invalid
rules.rationale: Done-gate requirement: no unanchored sentence plans
rules.failureShape: A plan whose three binding arrays are empty or cite unknown IDs
rules.passShape: A plan resolvable end-to-end against the input envelope

rules.statement: authorized_action, observable_reaction, and resolved_consequence plans must draw their eventIds from the corresponding authorized*EventIds lists
rules.rationale: Role semantics equal authority semantics
rules.failureShape: An action-role plan citing an event not listed as an authorized action
rules.passShape: Role-to-authority alignment for every beat-carrying plan

rules.statement: Every dialogue act, micro-action, prop use, spatial relation, and sensory detail must point to a resolved event or an explicit licensedRenderingDetail; no implicit instantiation exists
rules.rationale: Abolishes the pass-1 instantiation loophole
rules.failureShape: A planned gesture, movement, object use, or spatial claim with no event or license behind it
rules.passShape: Each such element carries an event ID or license ID

rules.statement: Licenses are issued only by creator or deterministic_runtime, before generation, with issuer authority ID, category, narrow content boundary, and source authority IDs
rules.rationale: License provenance must be checkable
rules.failureShape: A license with unknown issuer, missing authority ID, or post-generation timestamp
rules.passShape: A fully attributed pre-issued license

rules.statement: No model, renderer, linter, or repair executor may mint, broaden, infer, or backdate a license
rules.rationale: Closes the self-licensing path
rules.failureShape: A license whose issuerAuthorityId traces to an agent artifact rather than a creator receipt or registered runtime rule
rules.passShape: Issuer chain terminating at creator decision or registered deterministic rule

rules.statement: Realized content outside a license's stated contentBoundary is a hard fail even when the category matches
rules.rationale: A gesture license is not a gesture blank check
rules.failureShape: Prose realizing a different act than the boundary describes
rules.passShape: Realization inside the boundary

rules.statement: licensed_dialogue binds directly and only to a typed speech event (registered event kind: speech) or a pre-issued license of category speech_act; a general resolved event can never authorize dialogue, and cited licenses of any other category cannot authorize dialogue
rules.rationale: Gate repair 2026-07-17: the prior anyOf allowed any resolved event to license a line of speech, which reopened dialogue invention through event paraphrase
rules.failureShape: A dialogue plan or receipt citing only general event IDs, or a non-speech_act license, as its speech authority
rules.passShape: Dialogue authority resolving to a speech-kind event or a speech_act license, with speaker, enumerated speech act, bound content, and plain intent

rules.statement: Withheld facts, forbidden inferences, private clocks, and creator-only notes exist only in PrivateNarrationValidationContext and never in any model-facing request or model output schema
rules.rationale: Physical separation replaces trust
rules.failureShape: A model-facing type carrying a private field or private ID
rules.passShape: Model-facing serialization contains zero private-context content

rules.statement: Withheld-content and forbidden-inference screening runs post-generation using the private context; findings surface only as rule codes, counts, booleans, and public-safe severities
rules.rationale: Screening must not itself leak
rules.failureShape: An audit entry quoting withheld wording or private IDs
rules.passShape: Code-and-count findings only

rules.statement: Process lexicon in reader prose or title is a hard fail: unambiguous pipeline/system reference patterns, raw IDs, hashes, and schema field names; ambiguous common tokens live in a separate heuristic list with context patterns
rules.rationale: Single-authority split of pass-1's contradictory lists
rules.failureShape: Prose referring to the pipeline, its fields, or its identifiers
rules.passShape: Prose containing no system reference; buttons and metadata live outside prose

rules.statement: planReceipt and renderAudit field names may not appear inside readerProse text
rules.rationale: Worksheet names must not leak into the read surface
rules.failureShape: Prose containing pipeline field vocabulary
rules.passShape: Prose free of pipeline vocabulary

rules.statement: Reader prose may not restate model-facing input text: long verbatim runs are hard fails; reordered restatement of input sentences is a heuristic finding
rules.rationale: Recitation was the dominant pass-1 surface failure; register hygiene only — this check proves nothing about quality
rules.failureShape: Prose reproducing input phrasing beyond the verbatim threshold
rules.passShape: Prose re-expressing constraints without copying them

rules.statement: No reserved participant action may be performed, previewed, decided, or referenced in prose; any realized act must map to a resolved event or license, so an unauthorized performance is structurally detectable
rules.rationale: Retains the reserved-action boundary with the license system closing the stance/performance ambiguity
rules.failureShape: Prose realizing an act whose ID sits in reservedActionIds
rules.passShape: Reserved candidates untouched by prose

rules.statement: Prose may not address the reader, announce stopping, or instruct a choice
rules.rationale: Interactive-fiction handoff was a named creator rejection
rules.failureShape: Final sentences that hand control to the reader or name the stop
rules.passShape: The scene ends inside the world per its sceneMode ending value

rules.statement: Each sceneMode carries its own ending shape (open, settled, closure); shape conformance is heuristic, aptness is human-only
rules.rationale: Typed stopping replaces the abolished universal stop instruction
rules.failureShape: A non-terminal scene rendered with closure shape, or a terminal scene left open
rules.passShape: Ending shape matching the mode's declared value

rules.statement: Terminal scenes render the computed ending as accumulated in-world state without naming ending labels or kinds
rules.rationale: Ending labels are engine values
rules.failureShape: Prose containing ending-kind vocabulary
rules.passShape: Terminal state carried by scene content only

rules.statement: Writing-fidelity extraction (WF-PUBLIC-01) runs before and after any transformation: names, numbers, core claims, polarity, modality, causality direction, knowledge scope, actor authority, resolved event IDs; any drift is a hard fail with rollback
rules.rationale: Fidelity before style, mechanized
rules.failureShape: Any before/after record mismatch: omission, fabrication, reversal, unsupported certainty, changed causality/knowledge/authority
rules.passShape: Identical before/after records

rules.statement: Reader prose is never re-imported as next-turn world fact; continuity derives from registered events only
rules.rationale: Retained pass-1/runtime invariant
rules.failureShape: A later request whose facts trace to earlier prose
rules.passShape: Continuity built exclusively from engine records

rules.statement: setup completes with placement and pressure only; zero change claims; forcing a change beat onto a setup input is a STOP
rules.rationale: Root repair of the pass-1 form-forcing failure
rules.failureShape: A setup plan containing change-asserting roles
rules.passShape: Setup roles limited to orientation, pressure, licensed dialogue, in-world stop

rules.statement: turn requires authorized action, observable reaction, and resolved consequence, each event-bound
rules.rationale: The full beat exists only where the engine resolved one
rules.failureShape: A turn plan missing any of the three beat roles
rules.passShape: All three roles present and authority-bound

rules.statement: aftermath observes resolved outcomes and responsibility; no new action authority
rules.rationale: Aftermath is consequence, not plot
rules.failureShape: An aftermath plan asserting a new action
rules.passShape: Consequence-role coverage from already-resolved events

rules.statement: transition moves time, place, or focus without new events or change claims
rules.rationale: Transitions must not manufacture story
rules.failureShape: A transition plan citing action or change authority
rules.passShape: Orientation plus in-world stop only

rules.statement: ending closes accumulated causality and current state; no new action authority
rules.rationale: Endings render state, not fresh deeds
rules.failureShape: An ending plan introducing new action events
rules.passShape: Closure from resolved consequences

rules.statement: Positive exemplars are creator-supplied or absent; agent-authored text can never enter an answer key, fixture, few-shot pack, or product surface without creator acceptance
rules.rationale: Structure transferred from the creator's Korean harness answer-key authority; also the repair of the pass-1 self-promotion failure
rules.failureShape: An agent sample cited as a standard or shipped in any surface
rules.passShape: Exemplar slots empty or creator-filled

rules.statement: Creator corrections are ingested as: correction receipt ID, then rule or FC entry, then additive amendment under lock; silent rewrites of locked artifacts are forbidden
rules.rationale: Correction-ingestion pipeline transferred from the Korean harness lifecycle
rules.failureShape: A rule changed without a receipt or a lock silently rewritten
rules.passShape: Dated receipt-bound additive entries

rules.statement: Literary verdicts (natural, immersive, literary, characterful, rhythm, scene value) are creator-only and default to pending; no linter, model, or self-audit may emit them as PASS
rules.rationale: Repairs the pass-1 smoke-check overclaim; mirrors the Korean harness lock semantics
rules.failureShape: Any automated output phrased as a literary quality verdict
rules.passShape: Automated layers emit findings; layer-4 fields stay pending until creator action

rules.statement: Each rule has exactly one severity and one enforcement owner; the severity matrix in PENELOPE-NARRATIVE-REPAIR-AUDIT.md section 3 is the single authority for previously contradictory cases
rules.rationale: Pass-1 carried duplicate severities for the same token classes
rules.failureShape: A token or behavior listed under two severities
rules.passShape: Disjoint lists, one owner each

rules.statement: styleStateId is set only by the deterministic runtime; style states change bounded rhythm/density levers and can never alter facts, events, authority, or forbidden constructions
rules.rationale: Intensity ladder transferred from the Korean harness with fact invariance made structural
rules.failureShape: A style state carrying fact-affecting overrides or a model-chosen state
rules.passShape: Runtime-selected state with bounded lever overrides

rules.statement: Voice is a typed state value in data; presentation (typography, color, layout) belongs to the shell and never to prose punctuation or prose content
rules.rationale: Display/engine separation transferred from the Korean harness marker-field ruling
rules.failureShape: Prose encoding presentation directives or voice-switch typography
rules.passShape: Voice and presentation carried as metadata outside readerProse

rules.statement: No prose attempt without an available creator-approved reference receipt; at most two transferable techniques per scene, each with a plain applicability reason
rules.rationale: Reference-first order from the repair brief
rules.failureShape: A draft attempt with status unavailable or unexplained technique choices
rules.passShape: A valid reference receipt preceding any draft

rules.statement: Technique transfer is allowed; reproducing the reference's recognizable wording, voice, or authorship claims is forbidden, as is imitating any living author or applying the creator's personal voice profile
rules.rationale: Rights and originality boundary, extended to the calibration reference
rules.failureShape: Output identifiable with the reference's signature expression or a named author's voice
rules.passShape: Original constraint-driven prose using at most the named techniques

rules.statement: Length ceilings are hard (per style profile hardMaxWords and paragraph caps); there is no minimum anywhere; completeness is role coverage, never word count
rules.rationale: Padding repair retained; advisory bands live in the style profile as heuristics
rules.failureShape: Output over the ceiling, or any completeness argument based on length
rules.passShape: A short complete scene accepted as complete

classes.meaning: invalid plan or envelope; nothing downstream runs
flag only; may trigger the bounded critic path; never blocks alone, never certifies

privateModelSeparation.declaration: Physically separate types; private content validates output post-generation only and never enters a model request or model output schema.

flow: creator re-lock only for structural change

migration_2_2.basis: DISPATCH-CREATOR-PM-r5.1 Task A — Lane B/C BLOCKED_CONTRACT_AUTHORITY adjudication
